nameMeanings.name: Palmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English palmer(e) ‘palmer, pilgrim to the Holy Land’ (Anglo-Norman French palmer, Old French pa(l)mer, paum(i)er), so called from the palm branch carried by such pilgrims. The term was also used to denote an itinerant monk who traveled from shrine to shrine under a vow of poverty. This surname is also common in Ireland, where it has been recorded from the 13th century onward.
Irish: when not of English origin (see 1 above), a surname adopted for Gaelic Ó Maolfhoghmhair (see Milford ), the name of an ecclesiastical family.
Swedish (mainly Palmér): ornamental name formed with palm ‘palm tree’ + the suffix -ér (a derivative of Latin -erius) or -er (from German).

variants.name: Milford
meanings: English (mainly Devon): habitational name from any of the many places called Milford, deriving from Old English myln ‘mill’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish (Antrim and Galway): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolfhoghmhair ‘descendant of Maolgfhoghmhair’, a personal name meaning ‘chief of harvest’. The Irish name was first Anglicized as Mullover, which was later assimilated to Milford. See also Palmer .

variants.name: Palmiere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Dante, Enrico, Nicola, Salvatore, Vittorio.

variants.name: Palmore
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Palmer   or an Americanized form of South German   Balmer  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William Palmer was born in 1250, in Gloucestershire, England, his father, John Palmer II, was 26 and his mother, Amicia De Clopton, was 21. He married Constance Bilney in 1272, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1310, in England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Palmer est né en 1250, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, John Palmer II, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Amicia De Clopton, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Constance Bilney en 1272, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1310, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Palmer 1250 in Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Palmer II 26 und seine Mutter Amicia De Clopton 21. Er hat 1272 in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Constance Bilney geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1310 im Alter von 60 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Palmer è nato il nel 1250 nel Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, John Palmer II, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Amicia De Clopton, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Constance Bilney nel 1272, nel Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1310, nel England, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Palmer(이)가 1250년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Palmer II(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Amicia De Clopton(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1272년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 Constance Bilney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1310년 영국에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
